theme.name: broadway_boogie

motif.name: boogie_cell
motif.notes: Decouple pass (v3): reshaped from flat [0.5]*8 to a shaped 8-beat (2-bar) cell now that only melody (always) and harmony (accumulation/peak sections, via the 'stressed' >=median filter) consume it — the bass no longer reads this cell at all. Contour unchanged: root-3rd-5th-6th-b7-6th-5th-3rd, diatonic F Mixolydian. Melody onsets: 0, 1.5, 2, 3, 4, 4.5, 5, 6 — the off-beat pushes at 1.5/4.5 are the swing carriers. Harmony 'stressed' filter (median dur = 1.0) keeps slots 0,2,3,6,7: onsets 0, 2, 3, 5, 6 with durations 1.5/1/1/1/2 — five varied-length comp chords per 2-bar cycle, ALL on the beat by design, because the harmony path never receives swing (engine plumbing gap, logged) and on-beat chords are identical in straight and swung time. Off-beat comp onsets would flam against the swung melody.

theme.notes: Inspired by Mondrian's Broadway Boogie Woogie (1942-43) and its grid of primary color blocks on a yellow ground. Key decisions: F Mixolydian means I7 = F-A-C-Eb — dominant-seventh quality on the tonic. This chord never resolves. It is a color field, not a function. The flat-7 (Eb) gives momentum without resolution anxiety. No bVII, no IV, no modulation. The grid IS the composition; only density, register, and tempo change. Correction: II and V as originally notated implied major qualities requiring B-natural and E-natural, neither in F Mixolydian. Diatonic ii (Gm) and v (Cm) are minor -- use lowercase Roman numerals throughout, plus iii° (Adim/half-dim, passing) and vi (Dm). The vi-ii-v-I descending-fourth chain is the primary harmonic vehicle for high-density movements: functional-sounding motion built entirely from diatonic chords, so it drives without ever resolving to a leading tone. bVII (Eb) is reserved for lower-density/color-block movements (Mondrian color-field concept). DECOUPLE PASS (v3): swing_ratio REMOVED — verified against schemas.py: ThemeModel is extra="allow" and no code reads any theme-level swing field, so the v2 fix was a dead letter (the render was still straight). Swing is a SECTION-level field (SectionModel.swing, 0.0-1.0, remapped internally via remap_swing_ratio); every section in piece v3 now sets swing: 0.34 (public scale; = 0.67 internal triplet ratio after remap_swing_ratio). It reaches the melody path only — bass and harmony never receive swing (gap logged as deferred) — which is survivable because the steady bass figures and the kept harmony onsets are all on-beat.
